description: The spec asked to run analyse() inside a Web Worker via DOMParser, but DOMParser is [Exposed=Window] and does not exist in Worker scope.
resolution: Run analyse() on the main thread (native DOMParser, namespace-aware, zero parser deps). Yield once via setTimeout(0) before parsing so a loading state paints. For very large files, the upgrade path is a fast-xml-parser worker; pretty-print/DOM work must stay on main thread.

description: prettyXml() called XMLSerializer.serializeToString(el) on every element just to read its opening tag, but that re-serializes the whole subtree. On a 25MB file the line diff hung for minutes.
resolution: Build the opening tag directly from el.tagName + attributes (O(1) per element => O(n) total). Also render the line diff non-blocking (setTimeout) and collapse long unchanged runs to a fold row, capped at 20k rows, or the DOM alone freezes the browser.

description: Tried to surface undefined namespace prefixes as a soft warning (undefinedNamespaces). But per XML namespaces spec an undeclared prefix is a well-formedness error, so DOMParser rejects the whole document - it can never be a warning on an otherwise-valid file.
resolution: Detect the parsererror and throw XmlParseError with line/column + the offending prefix. Do not expect undefinedNamespaces to populate on a parseable doc.

content: Spec lists 1/0 under boolean, but integer is checked first so bare '1'/'0' resolve to integer (a 0/1/2 column reads integer, not boolean). Only word forms true/false/yes/no are boolean.

name: analyse() streams via saxes in an inlined Web Worker
description: analyse() was rewritten from DOMParser to a saxes streaming walk (stack of frames; opentag/text/cdata/closetag events) so it has no DOM dependency and runs off-thread. It runs in parse.worker.ts (imported '?worker&inline' so it's a Blob worker that works under file://) via parseClient.ts (one lazy worker, requests matched to replies by incrementing id). AnalysisResult's Map/Set cross postMessage via structured clone unchanged; XmlParseError is flattened to {message,line,column} and rebuilt on the main thread. Unbound-prefix/malformed input: saxes 'error' event sets a captured error (with parser.line/column) that's thrown after close. Supersedes the old 'DOMParser is main-thread only' blocker (mx-673a98). Note: prettyXml in diff.ts still uses DOMParser on the main thread.

name: formatXml: lossless streaming pretty-printer, off-thread
description: core/format.ts formatXml() replaces the old DOMParser-based prettyXml in diff.ts (which is now 'export { formatXml as prettyXml }'). Streams with a non-namespaced saxes parser (lenient: no xmlns validation, returns input unchanged on error) and a frame stack: elements with only text collapse to one line, otherwise multi-line indented; comments, CDATA and PIs are preserved (the DOM version dropped them); mixed content collapses direct text to one block. Runs in parse.worker.ts via formatInWorker(); EditorTab's auto-format-on-open and Format button are now async worker calls. With this, no DOMParser/XMLSerializer remain anywhere in src — parse+format are fully off-thread. lineDiff still calls formatXml on the main thread inside DiffTab (not yet worker-ized).
